title: Who is Strong? One Who Conquers Their Inclination
title_zh: 谁是强者?克服自己欲望的人
author: Torah Light Project
source_quote_translation: Who is strong? One who conquers their inclination
target_audience: Chinese Mandarin speakers learning about Judaism
cultural_connections: Confucian concept of 克己复礼 (restraining self, returning to propriety)
Mencius's four sprouts (四端) of moral virtue
Daodejing Chapter 33: 胜人者有力,胜己者强
Concept of 君子 (junzi - person of noble character)

bibliography.title: Talmud, Tractate Yoma
bibliography.topic: The necessity of the yetzer hara

bibliography.title: Talmud, Tractate Kiddushin
bibliography.topic: Dragging the yetzer to the study hall

notes: This article draws extensive parallels between Jewish and Chinese philosophical traditions on self-mastery. Emphasizes the universal nature of the teaching while remaining grounded in Jewish sources. Chinese cultural connections include Confucian self-restraint (克己), Mencius's moral cultivation, and Daoist concepts of inner strength.
